text: Research from the University of Texas at Austin and KPMG, which analyzed 1.4 million workplace AI interactions, found that so-called “power users” don’t just use AI more. They use it differently. Instead of treating it as a shortcut, they approach it as a thinking partner. Read more at the link in the bio.

Should you find yourself within the walls of this Florentine apartment, whose very being is a Renaissance name-dropper’s dream, there will be much to steal your gaze.⁣
⁣
Within the private 2,476-square-foot residence in Palazzo Uguccioni—built for one Giovanni Uguccioni in the mid-1500s and most likely designed by Raphael, though Michelangelo and Palladio are also in the mix as possible architects—intricately carved wood paneling, richly textured wallpapers in regal reds and golds, and stone figurines on plinths lend the rooms gallery-like grandeur.⁣
⁣
But beyond all that, there is one feature that sums up the majesty of this apartment. Two red wingback chairs—custom-made by Gucci—stand poised on a platform. Before them, a pair of glazed doors have been flung open to reveal a front-seat view of the Gothic turrets and arches of the Palazzo Vecchio.⁣
⁣
Tap the link in our bio to read more. ⁣
📸: Building Heritage

This new vacation home by Studio Schicketanz is set amidst an enclave of historic logging cabins in Big Sur, California. To honor the scale of the surrounding cottages, the architects divided the compact structure into three linked volumes sheathed in fire-resistant materials and topped one portion of the roof with an edible garden. Read more at the link in the bio.⁣
Photo: Joe Fletcher

The switch between the individualistic elements of being a contributor and thoughtfulness behind being a team leader, can be difficult for many new bosses. The newly promoted leader is still fixated on their own performance. The only thing that’s changed in their mind is that they now have a team to do their bidding, allowing them to accomplish more and get the credit. This is a leadership disaster waiting to happen. Read more at the link in the bio.

A consumer advocacy organization is warning certain borrowers that they are running out of time to consolidate their student loans. If they miss an upcoming deadline, the group said, these borrowers could permanently lose access to affordable repayment options and eventual student loan forgiveness.⁣
⁣
Read more at the link in the bio. (📸: Samuel Corum via Getty Images)

“Being able to adapt to change,” Louis Carr, President of BET says. “Being able to reinvent yourself. Being able to understand what’s next”

Louis Carr has been at BET for more than 30 years. In December 2025, he was named president of the legacy cable network. He joined Forbes senior writer Jabari Young on The Enterprise Zone to discuss longevity in today’s workplace, his career at BET, and dive into his investment portfolio.

See the full interview at Forbes.com

As the creator economy scales, viral creator Yinka Lawanson (@lingandlamb) emphasizes a foundational question: do you truly love the process—or just the results? At the 2026 World Governments Summit, he breaks down authenticity, long-term growth, and why mastering the business side is essential for creators. Presented by @worldgovsummit⁣
#WorldGovSummit | #WGS2026

Formation Bio CEO Ben Liu is convinced that drug discovery isn’t the bottleneck in getting medicines to market—it’s going through clinical trials. Now he’s raised some $615 million from top investors to buy stalled drugs and use AI to stress test them faster.⁣
⁣
Tap the link in our bio to read the full story. ⁣
📸: Guerin Blask for Forbes

Marius quit his job with no plan, joined Antler to find the right co-founders, and ended up building Peec.ai.⁣
⁣
Now the company helps brands show up in AI search when people ask the questions that drive buying decisions.⁣
⁣
In just over a year, Peec.ai has grown to 50+ employees and raised $29 million across four funding rounds—riding the next wave of search.

One thing about @CyrusVeyssi: You can always count on them to bring the Pepto. 🙌

Listen to their full #ForbesTalksShop interview at the link in bio, or wherever you get your podcasts.
Photos: Getty Images

Nick and Bob have built brands like Dorn Rose, a sleep and wellness company, and Hears, a premium earplug brand for music and live events, scaling both across D2C, retail and B2B.⁣
⁣
What looks like momentum now is really years of testing products, refining positioning and figuring out what actually works behind the scenes.⁣
⁣
Anyone can start a brand. Few know how to scale one.

For years, Kim Rivers has been building one of the most powerful companies in cannabis while emerging as a key voice shaping its future.⁣
⁣
As the CEO behind Trulieve’s rise into a billion-dollar business, she’s navigated an industry still operating illegally at the federal level.⁣
⁣
She’s been a leader in the lobbying effort to persuade President Trump to issue an executive order to reschedule marijuana. Once marijuana is reclassified, it will transform the entire industry. ⁣
⁣
Read more at the link in bio.
